summary: Excellent soundboard recording captures a short but scorching Texas set on 8/30/85 with a standout “The Other One”.
review: DeadCoMule’s five‑star review praises this August 30, 1985 performance as an "exquisite, leathery, piping hot Texas special" – a concise six‑song first set that stays consistently strong and even highlights a fiery 5:40 stretch of “The Other One.” The SBD source is described as having real balance and clarity, making the heat of the night audible in the mix. Overall sentiment is overwhelmingly positive.

ai_rating.rationale: One detailed 5‑star review lauds both musical performance and recording quality; only a trivial zero‑star thank‑you note adds little weight, yielding a high rating with moderate confidence due to limited sample size.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Excellent SBD recording noted for real balance and clarity; crisp sound that conveys the heat of the performance.

show_quality.setlist_flow: A quick, six‑song first set (only three pre‑drums) that remains consistently strong; tight pacing makes the short show feel purposeful and engaging.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Described his guitar tone as 'Tiger purring and growling', indicating a powerful, expressive performance.
